latest.title: Two tests GPs can soon offer to help spot endometriosis
latest.summary: GPs may soon be able to offer two non-invasive tests to help detect endometriosis. The aim is to shorten long waits for diagnosis.
latest.details: GPs may soon be able to offer two non-invasive tests to help detect endometriosis. Experts hope the tests will make diagnosis faster and easier for patients.

latest.title: Samsung profits jump 1,800% as AI chip sales soar
latest.summary: Samsung said profits rose sharply as demand for AI chips increased. The figures are an early signal before the company publishes full results.
latest.details: Samsung said profits rose sharply as demand for AI chips increased. Investors watch the update because Samsung is a major supplier in the global chip market.

latest.title: E-bike injury payouts top £110m and push up insurance premiums
latest.summary: E-bike injury claims in the UK have led to large payouts. Insurers say the cost of claims is one factor pushing up premiums.
latest.details: E-bike injury claims in the UK have led to payouts of more than £110m. Insurers say large claims are feeding into higher premiums for drivers.

latest.title: Why some African nations are turning down Trump aid money
latest.summary: Some African governments are resisting new US aid deals because they come with conditions. The article explains the trade-off between support and political pressure.
latest.details: Some African governments are resisting new US aid deals because they come with conditions. The story focuses on why financial support can also create political pressure.

latest.title: Robots available for rent: But what can they do?
latest.summary: Hospitals and other workplaces are renting robots for practical tasks. The article looks at what these machines can realistically do today.
latest.details: Hospitals and other workplaces are renting robots for practical tasks. One example is a robot that moves medical supplies around hospitals.
